data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this .NET project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to:

1. First, explore the project structure to understand what we're working with
2. Set up and run code coverage
3. Analyze the results

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

data.content: Good — the project already has `coverlet.collector` referenced. Let me run tests with coverage collection.
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage enabled using coverlet (which is already referenced in the test project), then analyze the results.

I'll use `dotnet test` with coverage collection and output in Cobertura format so I can parse it.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-b49341de80b64f43bc69a53dd3c59053 && dotnet test --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --results-directory ./TestResults -- DataCollectionRunSettings.DataCollectors.DataCollector.Configuration.Format=cobertura 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with Cobertura coverage", "initial_wait": 60}
